question: Define the projection of a vector onto another vector in an inner product space. State the formula for the projection of vector u onto vector v.
expectedPoints: Definition: The projection of u onto v is the vector component of u that lies in the direction of v.
The formula is proj_v(u) = (<u, v> / <v, v>) * v
This scalar projection coefficient is the inner product <u, v> divided by the squared norm of v, <v, v>.

question: Explain why the vector (u - proj_v(u)) is orthogonal to the vector v. Use the properties of the inner product in your explanation.
expectedPoints: To show orthogonality, the inner product <(u - proj_v(u)), v> must equal zero.

question: Describe the process of finding the best approximation of a vector u in an inner product space V by a vector in a finite-dimensional subspace W. Your answer must include the role of an orthogonal basis for W and the general projection formula.
expectedPoints: The best approximation is the orthogonal projection of u onto the subspace W.
An orthogonal basis {w1, w2, ..., wk} for W simplifies the calculation, avoiding the need to solve a system of normal equations.
The projection formula is proj_W(u) = (<u, w1>/<w1, w1>)*w1 + (<u, w2>/<w2, w2>)*w2 + ... + (<u, wk>/<wk, wk>)*wk.
This projection is unique and minimizes the distance ||u - w|| for all w in W.
